bad_pattern: Auto-generated reports get ignored; unnecessary automation can introduce expensive mistakes.
better_pattern: Keep manual contact with the market when the manual task is cheap and informative.

lecture_rule: Review to stay immersed, not to rewrite rules from tiny samples.
bad_pattern: A 20-day live sample gets a new Thursday-loss story attached to it.
better_pattern: Compare the live sample to the original evidence before changing rules.

lecture_rule: Use CBT-style tools to manage reactions rather than trying harder.
bad_pattern: Trader's block, revenge trading, staying in a position only because it is down.
better_pattern: Write the feared outcome, rehearse the loss limit, and ask what advice you would give another trader.

lecture_rule: Monitor the edge, not every tick of P&L.
bad_pattern: Watching money changes in real time drives unnecessary action.
better_pattern: Use thesis, risk, and edge metrics as decision triggers.

lecture_rule: Do not set impossible outcome targets.
bad_pattern: Trying to force an edge to meet an arbitrary P&L target.
better_pattern: Evaluate whether the realized outcome is consistent with expected edge and risk.
